description_long: This object is a delicate, wireframe representation of a flower bouquet, featuring two distinct blossoms. The left blossom has five broad, rounded petals arranged radially, with a visible central structure and a thin, curved stem. The right blossom is more intricate, with numerous overlapping, narrow petals forming a dense, spherical cluster. Both flowers are connected by slender, twisting stems, and a few elongated, ovate leaves are visible near the base. The wire material is multicolored, with hues of blue, purple, green, and silver, giving the object a whimsical, airy appearance. The structure is open and lightweight, with much of its volume composed of empty space.
description: A delicate, multicolored wireframe bouquet with two distinct flowers—one with broad radial petals and another with a dense, overlapping cluster—connected by slender stems and ovate leaves.
description_view_0: The front view shows both flowers clearly: the left flower with five broad petals and the right flower with a dense, tangled cluster of narrow petals, both connected by thin stems and visible leaves.
description_view_1: From the side, the dense, spherical flower dominates the view, with the radial-petaled flower partially obscured and the stems appearing more compact.
description_view_2: This angle highlights the spatial separation between the two flowers, with the radial-petaled flower on the left and the dense cluster on the right, both connected by twisting stems.
description_view_3: The rear view emphasizes the wireframe structure of the dense flower, with the radial-petaled flower mostly hidden and the stems and leaves more visible.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals of the left flower are arranged radially in a single whorl, with each petal spaced evenly around the center. The right flower features a dense, overlapping, and somewhat spiral arrangement of narrow petals, creating a spherical cluster with no clear symmetry.
